beats.speaker: Doug
beats.text: "Brother. You need cash to sit down."

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: "Yeah... yeah."
The chips glitter. You do not have chip money. You hate chip money for this.
Folding table. Six chairs. Chips already stacked. Someone you don't know nurses a bourbon in the corner.

beats.speaker: Doug
beats.text: "$20 buy-in, brother. Low stakes. Just for fun."
The stranger does not think it's just for fun.
You're up $30. Table laughs. Stranger doesn't.

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: "Maybe I AM good at this."
Stranger raises. Pot swells like a bruise.

choices.text: "I'm feeling lucky" — push for big pot

beats.text: All in. You draw cards. The stranger has a face the Mona Lisa would envy.

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: "...flush?"

beats.speaker: Stranger
beats.text: "Full house."
Doug winces. "Brutal. Brutal stuff. One more?"

beats.speaker: Stranger
beats.text: "If you need a bigger game, talk to me after."

beats.speaker: Stranger
beats.text: "Dentist's office. After hours. $500 minimum."

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: "A dentist's office."

beats.speaker: Stranger
beats.text: "There's a reason they scream when they pull teeth."
Four men, one woman, one folding chair too few. Cigar smoke. A vending machine that only takes $100s.
You read the table. Woman to your left is tired. Stranger never blinks. Man in cowboy hat has never bluffed once in his life.

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: "All in."
The room goes quiet. The dentist chair creaks. Cowboy hat folds. Tired lady folds. Stranger calls.
You flip your cards. He flips his. You win.

beats.speaker: Stranger
beats.text: "Come back when you're serious."
He closes the matchbook he was writing the address on. You walk away feeling somehow judged by a man who bankrolls underground card games.

beats.speaker: Doug
beats.text: "Brother. The table tonight is SERIOUS. Real estate guys. A dentist. Someone named Gary."

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: "Who's Gary?"

beats.speaker: Doug
beats.text: "You don't ask about Gary."
You lose everything in 40 minutes. Gary doesn't even look at you.

beats.speaker: Doug
beats.text: "Brutal. Brutal stuff."
You walk back to the minivan. You practice what you'll tell Karen. None of it holds up.

leave.text: You stand up. Doug nods. 'Any time, brother.' The stranger does not nod.
